summary:
The mother requires screening for autoimmune disorders due to her fetus having congenital heart block; primary tests include checking for APLA and SLE markers because these can cause similar issues prenatally through antibody transmission from mother to child. \boxed{C}

The summary concisely captures the need for maternal screenings focusing on specific autoimmune indicators linked directly to causing congenital heart blocks observed clinically in fetuses. It maintains relevance strictly around identifying risk factors based upon known associations between certain maternal health statuses impacting developing hearts negatively during pregnancy stages
